['Playing Subway Surfers is an exciting and enjoyable experience that feels both relaxing and thrilling at the same time. The game is not too difficult, especially at the beginning, which makes it welcoming for new players. When you first start running, the speed is slow and manageable. This gives you enough time to understand the controls and get used to swiping left, right, up, and down. Because of this smooth start, players do not feel overwhelmed.', 'I found Subway Surfers to be incredibly disappointing and, at times, almost atrocious in terms of overall quality. The gameplay is painfully repetitive and lacks any real depth or innovation. After just a few minutes, every run feels identical and mind-numbingly monotonous. There is no meaningful strategy or skill progression, only endless, mechanical swiping. The excessive advertisements and aggressive in-app purchases feel intrusive and greedy, completely ruining the experience.', 'Subway Surfers is a free-to-play endless runner mobile game where you control a character running through train tracks, dodging trains and obstacles while collecting coins, power-ups, and missions. You swipe up/down/left/right to jump, slide, and switch lanes. The story is very simple — you’re a graffiti artist escaping an inspector and his dog']
